info.name: IA News Pipeline
info.description:
Story S4.1: Postman collection for the two frozen `service` endpoints (architecture §5.2). The collection-level pre-request script computes `X-Pipeline-Timestamp` and `X-Pipeline-Signature` (HMAC-SHA256, hex, `sha256=` prefix) exactly as implemented in `service/IaNewsPipeline.Api/Security/HmacRequestValidator.cs`, so the evaluator never has to hand-compute a signature.

Setup: import this collection and the accompanying `ia-news-pipeline.postman_environment.json`, select that environment, fill in `PIPELINE_SHARED_SECRET` with the value from your local `.env` (matches `PIPELINE_SHARED_SECRET` in `docker-compose.yml` / `.env.example`), and run the three requests top to bottom: 1) Generate Post — Happy Path, 2) Generate Post — Bad Signature, 3) Get Job Status.

Out of scope (see story Dev Notes): the WordPress webhook receiver (§5.1) is service→WordPress only, never called from Postman.

exec: // Collection-level HMAC signer (architecture.md #5, D4; matches HmacRequestValidator.cs).
// Skipped entirely for GET requests: GET /api/jobs/{id} is intentionally unauthenticated
// per architecture.md #5.2 ("GET /api/jobs/{id} — no auth (POC; documented)") and Program.cs
// (no HmacRequestValidator is wired into that route). Adding signature headers there would be
// harmless server-side but would misrepresent the contract, so this script does not run for GET.
// CRITICAL: resolve {{variables}} in the body BEFORE signing. Postman's pre-request script runs
// before variable substitution happens on the outgoing request, so pm.request.body.raw still
// contains unresolved '{{...}}' placeholders at this point. Signing that unresolved text would
// produce a signature that never matches the bytes actually transmitted. pm.variables.replaceIn()
// resolves them explicitly first. This is defensive even for today's static bodies, so the script
// doesn't silently break the moment someone parameterizes a body later.
// CryptoJS is available globally in the Postman sandbox (no require/pm.require needed).
// .toString() on an HMAC WordArray defaults to lowercase hex, matching .NET's
// Convert.ToHexStringLower used server-side — do not add an uppercase transform.
pm.test('Response has job_id and status_url', function () {

request.description:
Signed by the collection-level pre-request script using {{PIPELINE_SHARED_SECRET}}. Expects `202 Accepted` with body `{ "job_id": "<guid>", "status_url": "/api/jobs/<guid>" }` (relative path). `job_id` is captured into the `job_id` collection variable so the "Get Job Status" request can reuse it without manual copy-paste.

The request body uses `{{article_url}}`, which defaults to a publicly reachable article URL that passed the S4.3 happy-path verification. Replace it with another public article URL when desired.

Note: `PublicUrlValidator` rejects loopback/localhost and private/reserved IP ranges.

exec: // Request-level script runs AFTER the collection-level script (Postman execution order is
// collection -> folder -> request), so this intentionally overwrites the correctly-signed
// headers with a signature computed against a hardcoded wrong secret. That makes this request
// deterministically 401 regardless of what the evaluator put in PIPELINE_SHARED_SECRET.

request.description: Demonstrates the `401` path deterministically: this request's own pre-request script re-signs the body with a hardcoded wrong secret (`"wrong-secret"`), so it fails regardless of the evaluator's real `PIPELINE_SHARED_SECRET`. Expects `401 Unauthorized` with an empty body (`Results.Unauthorized()` server-side — no body shape to assert).

exec: pm.test('Response has job_id and state', function () {
console.log('Job ' + pm.response.json().job_id + ' is in state: ' + pm.response.json().state);

request.description:
Unsigned by design — per architecture.md §5.2 ("GET /api/jobs/{id} — no auth (POC; documented)") and confirmed against Program.cs (this route has no HmacRequestValidator dependency). No signature headers are sent or required.

Uses the `job_id` collection variable captured by "Generate Post — Happy Path". Expects `200 OK` with body `{ "job_id", "state", "post_url"?, "error"? }`.

`state` values (service/IaNewsPipeline.Api/Jobs/JobStates.cs), in expected progression order:
- `queued` — accepted, waiting for the worker to pick it up off the queue
- `processing` — worker is extracting the article and generating content via OpenAI
- `publishing` — worker is POSTing the signed webhook to the WordPress receiver
- `published` — terminal success; `post_url` is populated with the live WordPress post URL
- `failed` — terminal failure; `error` is populated with a machine-readable reason (e.g. `invalid_url`, `source_not_found`, `source_not_article`, a receiver `401`/`422`, or a permanent OpenAI/network failure)

Re-run this request (or use Postman's Collection Runner / Runner delay) to poll until `state` reaches `published` or `failed`.
